question: Can I really change the way I think?
expected_answer: A: Yes. Your brain is capable of forming new thought habits. With awareness and practice, you can train your mind to think in ways that support you. Watch this short video on changing your thoughts to learn how it works. To understand WHY you think the way you do, explore this guided program on Conditioning.

question: How can mindfulness help with stress?
expected_answer: A: Mindfulness helps you pause and respond, rather than react. It gives your nervous system a chance to slow down and reset. Watch this short video on 5 ways you can be more mindful. Do these breathing exercises as a way of regulating your emotions.

question: Why won’t my mind stop racing?
expected_answer: A: A racing mind often means your brain is trying to solve, protect, or prepare — but it can leave you feeling restless. You don’t need to silence it, just slow it down. Watch this short video to calm a chattering mind to understand what’s going on. Do these breathing exercises as a way of regulating your emotions.

question: What thinking patterns make anger worse?
expected_answer: A: All-or-nothing thinking, assuming others’ intentions, or replaying what “should have happened” can all fuel anger. These thoughts may feel true, but often aren’t helpful. Watch this short video on thinking patterns that trigger anger. Explore these guided journaling exercises on Understanding your feelings.

question: Is it okay to set boundaries and be tolerant?
expected_answer: A: Absolutely. Tolerance doesn’t mean tolerating harm. You can be understanding and still say no. That’s healthy, respectful self-care. Watch this short video on Tolerance to explore more. Listen to this mini podcast on setting boundaries.

question: Why do misunderstandings happen so often?
expected_answer: A: Misunderstandings usually come from assumptions, unspoken expectations, or emotional filters. We all hear and interpret things differently. Watch this short video on 4 ways misunderstandings occur to shift from reacting to responding. Explore this guided program on Problems in Communication to find out more.

question: What happens when I finally face a hidden fear?
expected_answer: A: When you face a hidden fear with awareness and compassion, it often loses its grip on you. The stories that once kept you small start to shift. Watch this short video on how hidden fears shape our lives to explore how courage unfolds in small, quiet ways. Then explore this guided program on How fear shapes our lives.

question: How can I be more intelligent?
expected_answer: There are different types of intelligence: intellectual, emotional, of the body body, and a holistic intelligence which comes from self-knowledge. Listen to this podcast on Is our mind intelligent to understand this pattern with more compassion. You can also read this blog on Becoming more intelligent.

question: Why do I avoid asking myself certain questions?
expected_answer: A: Avoiding questions can be a way of protecting yourself from discomfort, uncertainty, or painful truths. It’s a survival instinct the mind often thinks, “If I don’t ask, I don’t have to feel.” Listen to this podcast on asking questions to explore how facing your questions can lead to clarity and emotional relief.

question: Why are people selfish?
expected_answer: We criticise others for things we don’t see in ourselves. In fact, all of us act from our self-interest, but are usually not aware of it. Explore this Guided program on Self interest to understand this better. Awareness of what your mind is doing allows you to live with integrity.

question: How can I live in peace?
expected_answer: Being at peace with ourselves is perhaps the ultimate expression of a happy mind. This can also help us live in peace with others. To begin, we need to understand what gets in the way of being at peace. Watch this short video on Being at peace. Explore this Guided program on Feeling at peace for more insights.

question: How can I feel peaceful?

question: How can I feel at peace within myself?

question: Why is it so hard to be kind to myself?
expected_answer: A: If you’ve grown up being too hard on yourself, kindness can feel unfamiliar—but you can learn it. Explore this Guided program on Feeling at peace for more insights.

question: How can I be content?
expected_answer: Feeling content needs self-awareness. It’s a feeling of peace and comes with a deep acceptance of who we are, what we have, the people in our life and our circumstances. Watch this short video on Feeling content. Then listen to this podcast on Feeling at peace.

question: Where can I find peace
expected_answer: If we want to feel at peace inside, how do we start? Where is this peace to be found? We often to our relationships or work or money to give us this feeling of comfort. But perhaps we are looking in the wrong place and real peace lies within ourselves - we just have to connect with it. Watch this short video on Finding peace.

question: Why is there so much corruption?
expected_answer: Everywhere in the world we see corruption - in some cases it is hidden, and in other places more overt. Explore these guided programs on Self-Interest and Conditioning to find out more, and live with integrity.

question: How can I figure out who I really am?

question: I feel different to everyone around me. Is that normal?
expected_answer: All of us have different identities which are a blend of all our conditioning influences including our parents, our culture, our religion and national identity. question: Why is there so much conflict in the world?
expected_answer: The conflict we see between people and between countries is often rooted in identity. We all have different identities we become attached to and these shape who we think we are, our opinions and beliefs. We think we are right and others are wrong and conflict begins. Explore this Guided program on Identity to understand how identities form without our own awareness, and how we can live with more intelligence. This video explores how we could end war.

question: Why can’t I calm my thoughts when I sit still?
expected_answer: It can feel really frustrating when your mind won’t cooperate, especially when you’re trying to find peace. You’re not alone in this; stillness takes practice, not perfection. You’re not failing at meditation just because it feels noisy. Start with the short video on Calm a Chattering Mind to gently observe what’s coming up. Then explore the Develop a Calm Mind guided program to learn how to let thoughts pass without judgment.

question: How do I start meditating if I’ve never done it before?
expected_answer: Starting anything new brings up nerves, it’s totally natural to feel uncertain. The fact that you’re even curious is already a powerful beginning. Starting something new can feel uncomfortable at first. You’re already doing great by just being curious. Read the blog on How to Meditate to learn the basics. Then explore the Tips for a Meditation Practice guided program to build a consistent practice that suits you.

question: What’s the point of meditation?
expected_answer: In a world that moves so fast, it’s easy to feel like you’ve lost touch with yourself. Meditation offers a quiet return to what matters. Sometimes life moves too fast, and it’s hard to hear your own thoughts. Meditation helps you come back to yourself. Begin with the short video on Live in the Moment to understand its purpose. Then explore the guided program What is Meditation to learn how it builds focus, peace, and clarity.

question: How does meditation help with stress?
expected_answer: Stress can feel like it’s hijacking your entire body. Meditation gives you a way to slow down and feel safe again. Stress can leave your body feeling tense and your mind overworked. Meditation helps create a pause and gives you room to breathe. Try the short video on Calm Your Mind With These Simple Tips and then listen to the Releasing Fear audio meditation to help your nervous system reset.

question: Why does my mind wander so much while meditating?
expected_answer: You’re not doing it wrong, this happens to everyone. In fact, noticing the wandering mind is part of the practice. Everyone’s mind wanders; it’s completely normal. What matters is that you’re noticing it. Watch the short video on Be Mindful to understand how to gently bring your attention back. Then try the Noticing Thoughts guided meditation to practice staying present.

question: I get bored while meditating. Is that normal?
expected_answer: Yes, boredom is part of the process. Your mind is simply adjusting to stillness after a lifetime of movement. Boredom is just your mind’s way of asking for something familiar. With time, stillness starts to feel like home. Watch the short video 5 Ways You Can Be More Mindful to bring variety to your practice. Then explore this guided program Tips for a Meditation Practice to stay engaged.

question: What’s a simple meditation I can do when I feel anxious?
expected_answer: Anxiety can feel loud and overwhelming, it’s okay to want relief fast. Meditation is a powerful way to ground yourself. Anxiety can make your thoughts race and your body feel wired. Meditation helps you slow down. Begin with the short video Calm a Chattering Mind to center your breath. Then try the Releasing Fear guided meditation to soothe your nervous system.

question: Can meditation help me sleep better?
expected_answer: Those racing thoughts at night can feel endless. Meditation can help signal your body and mind that it’s safe to rest. When your mind won’t stop at night, it’s a sign it needs a little care. Meditation creates the space for your body to relax deeply. Try the short video Feel at Peace before bed. Then listen to the Sea Inspired Meditation to unwind slowly. Listen to these beautiful soundscapes to feel more calm.

question: How long do I have to meditate for it to work?
expected_answer: You don’t need an hour of silence to benefit, what matters is presence, not perfection. Even 10 minutes can make a difference. What matters most is showing up, not how long you stay. Watch the short video Heart Breathing by Carolyn King for a quick reset. Then try the Tuning In guided meditation to gently build your routine.

question: Can I meditate while walking or moving?
expected_answer: Absolutely. Meditation is more about attention than posture. Moving meditation can be deeply grounding. Yes, meditation doesn’t always need stillness. Movement can be just as mindful. Try the Walk in Nature guided meditation to feel grounded as you move. You can also explore these Nature meditations to find out how you can meditate when out and about.

question: What if meditation brings up uncomfortable emotions?
expected_answer: That’s actually a healthy sign, it means something important is surfacing. You’re safe to feel it. Meditation creates space for what you’ve been holding in. Watch the short video How Can We Find Peace to better understand this. Then try the Releasing Anger guided meditation to help process emotional discomfort. Explore this Guided program on Meeting a feeling without naming it for more insights on how to deal with strong emotions.

question: How do I stay consistent with meditation?
expected_answer: It’s okay to miss a day, it doesn’t erase your progress. What matters is starting again. Small habits lead to lasting change. Watch the short video Be Mindful for easy ways to weave meditation into your day. Then explore Tips for a Meditation Practice to build a rhythm that works for you.

question: Can meditation help me understand myself better?
expected_answer: Yes, and that’s one of its most powerful gifts. Meditation helps you become your own friend. It helps you to tune in instead of tuning out. It creates space to notice your inner world. Begin with this short video on Meditation to get started. Then read the blog Understanding Ourselves to explore how reflection leads to self-awareness.

question: How do I address unconscious bias at work?

question: Why do I get defensive in conversations about racism?
expected_answer: That instinct to defend yourself is human. It often points to something beneath the surface you haven’t examined yet. The reaction is often shaped by past conditioning. question: How do I know if I have unconscious bias?

question: I feel judged because of my background at work. What should I do?

question: I’ve started to feel excluded in team conversations. Why does it hurt so much?

question: I struggle to manage my money wisely. Where do I start?

question: I feel anxious even when I have enough money. Why?
expected_answer: That fear isn’t about the numbers; it’s about safety and control. You deserve peace, not constant tension. Money anxiety can come from past conditioning. question: I feel ashamed that I care so much about money.

question: Why do I feel envious of people who earn more than me?

question: What is Happiness?

question: How can I handle negative thoughts?

question: How do I deal with constant mood swings?
expected_answer: A: Mood swings can be tough, but they’re often your mind’s way of signaling something deeper, like stress, hormones, lack of sleep, or emotional overload. Read this blog on how to track your moods daily to spot patterns. Prioritize rest, movement, and balanced meals. When emotions shift, pause and breathe. Practice these breathing exercises to feel more calm.

question: I don’t know why I feel a certain way.
expected_answer: A: Your emotions are your brain’s way of making sense of the world and protecting you. They’re shaped by your past experiences, beliefs, the current situation, and even your body’s signals, like hunger or tiredness. Every feeling has a purpose; it’s trying to tell you something. Explore these Guided journaling exercises on understanding feelings. Here’s a blog to help you understand your mind.

question: Why do I feel what I feel?
expected_answer: A: Your emotions are your brain’s way of making sense of the world and protecting you. They’re shaped by your past experiences, beliefs, the current situation, and even your body’s signals, like hunger or tiredness. Every feeling has a purpose; it’s trying to tell you something. Explore these Guided journaling exercises on understanding feelings. Here’s a blog to help you understand your mind.

question: How do I control my anger? How can I manage my anger?
expected_answer: A: Pause. Notice where the anger shows up in your body: tight jaw, racing heart, clenched fists. Take a breath. Step away if needed. Anger is a signal, not a solution. Once you’re calmer, you can express yourself in a way that helps you be heard and understood. Practice these breathing exercises to feel more calm. Have a look at this Feel better now section on tips to deal with anger now. Here is a video on thinking patterns that trigger anger.

question: How can I deal with loneliness?
expected_answer: A: Start by being kind to yourself. Reach out to someone, even with a simple message. Spend time doing something you enjoy or something that gives you purpose. You’re not alone in feeling lonely. Connection starts with small, brave steps, even with yourself. Watch this short video on the importance of connection. Listen to this audio meditation on Dealing with Loneliness. Explore this Guided program on Loneliness for more insights.

question: What should I do when I feel like crying but can’t explain why?
expected_answer: A: Let it out if you need to. It’s okay to cry! Sometimes your body knows what your mind hasn’t figured out yet. Crying helps release built-up emotions. You don’t need a reason to feel. Gently ask yourself: Have I been holding in stress, sadness, or tiredness? Watch this video on How to feel at peace. Explore this section on how to Manage your emotions.

question: What’s a healthy way to express sadness?
expected_answer: A: Write it out. Talk to someone you trust. Let yourself cry. Create something, art, music, movement. Give sadness space without judgment. It’s not a weakness but a signal. When you express sadness safely, it moves through you instead of staying stuck inside. Practice these breathing exercises to feel more calm. Use your online journal to write down what is making you sad.

question: How can I stop snapping at people I care about?
expected_answer: A: Often, snapping is a sign of built-up stress or unspoken emotions. Notice what triggers it. When you feel it coming, pause, take a breath, or count to five. Apologize if needed, and talk about what’s bothering you. Being honest and aware can help strengthen your relationships. Here is a guided meditation on releasing anger that might help. Explore this in-depth section on how to manage your emotions.

question: Why do I feel nothing sometimes?
expected_answer: A: Feeling numb is often your mind’s way of protecting you when things feel too much. It doesn’t mean you’re broken. Try doing gentle activities that reconnect you with your senses, warm showers, soft music, nature, or art. Practice these breathing exercises to feel more calm. Use your online journal to write down what is making you sad. Explore this section on managing emotions for detailed guidance.

question: How do I manage emotional triggers?
expected_answer: A: First, notice what triggers you, people, situations, or even thoughts. When it happens, pause and breathe. Remind yourself this is a reaction, not a reflection of who you are. Write about your triggers and work on healing the deeper hurt behind them. You can regain control. Use this journal section on the app to pen down your thoughts and feelings. Behind these emotional triggers could be past trauma which is unresolved. Listen to this podcast on Dealing with Past trauma.

question: Can emotions affect my physical health?
expected_answer: A: Yes, emotions and physical health are closely linked. Stress, anxiety, or sadness can lead to headaches, fatigue, sleep issues, or even stomach trouble. Likewise, a healthy body helps you manage emotions better. Treat both your body and mind with care, as they work as a team. Explore this section on managing emotions for more detailed insights.

question: How can I explain my emotions to others?
expected_answer: A: Start simple: “I’m feeling overwhelmed right now and I’m not sure why.” You don’t need to have all the answers. Focus on how the situation made you feel, not what someone did wrong. Use “I” statements and stay honest. Expressing emotions builds connection when done with care. Here is a short video by Coach Jondi Whitis on how connection is key. Explore the Guided program on Communication for more insights.

question: How can I calm my mind quickly?
expected_answer: A: It’s ok to feel how you feel. Take 3 slow, deep breaths. Breathe in for 4 seconds, hold for 4, and breathe out for 4. Focus only on your breath. Even one minute of this can calm your racing thoughts and help you reset. Watch this short video on how to calm your mind. Listen to this meditation on releasing negative thoughts.

question: Why is my mind always busy?
expected_answer: A: Our brain is built to solve problems and protect us, which is why it often feels like it’s always on. Giving it small breaks through quiet activities, like walking, meditation and journaling can help. Watch this short video on calming a chattering mind. Use the online journal to write down your thoughts and feelings - this can also help.

question: Why is my mind always preoccupied?
expected_answer: A: Our brain is built to solve problems and protect us, which is why it often feels like it’s always on. Giving it small breaks through quiet activities, like walking, meditation and journaling can help. Watch this short video on calming a chattering mind. Use the online journal to write down your thoughts and feelings - this can also help.

question: What’s the best way to start my day calmly?
expected_answer: A: Begin your day with a quiet moment, before checking your phone or screens. Take 5 minutes to breathe, stretch, or journal. This simple practice helps you feel more grounded and focused. It sets a calm tone for the day. Listen to this audio meditation to help you feel more balanced. Use the online journal to write down your thoughts. Listen to this podcast on living with peace.

question: Why does my mind race at night?
expected_answer: A: At night, your brain finally gets quiet after a busy day, and that’s when all the thoughts you’ve pushed aside start to surface. It’s normal, but it can feel overwhelming. Try writing down what’s on your mind in your online journal before bed, listening to calming music, or doing a simple breathing exercise. These small habits can help signal to your mind that it’s time to rest.

question: Can music calm my mind? And how?
expected_answer: A: Yes, music can calm your mind by slowing your heart rate, easing tension, and shifting your focus away from stress. Soft, slow-paced music or nature sounds can relax your nervous system and create a sense of peace. It acts like a gentle distraction, helping you feel more grounded and present. Find tunes that soothe you and make them part of your daily routine. Listen to these beautiful soundscapes to feel more calm.

question: Why do I feel calm one moment and overwhelmed the next?
expected_answer: A: That’s normal. Emotions shift quickly. When overwhelm hits, pause and check in. Breathe, slow down, and take one thing at a time. Practice this breathing exercise to regain clarity and calm. Listen to this short meditation to feel better quickly. Listen to these beautiful soundscapes to feel more calm.

question: How do I stop my mind from jumping to the worst-case scenario?
expected_answer: A: When your mind jumps to the worst-case scenario, it’s your brain trying to protect you from danger. But fear often exaggerates. Pause and ask: Is this thought 100% true? What else could happen? Remind yourself of the facts, not just the feelings. Taking deep breaths and grounding yourself in the present can help you stay calm. Watch this short video on how to calm your mind. Watch this video on how to overcome your fears. Listen to this short meditation on releasing fear.

question: How can I stay calm around difficult people?
expected_answer: A: Staying calm around difficult people starts by calming a reactive mind. Take deep breaths, pause before responding, and remind yourself that their behavior isn’t about you. Set boundaries when needed, and don’t let their mood control yours. Listen to this mini podcast on setting boundaries. You can’t change others, but you can choose to protect your peace. Listen to this short meditation to see people freshly. Explore this Guided program on a reactive mind for more insights. Practice this breathing exercise to regain clarity and calm.

question: Is it okay if I can’t calm down right away?
expected_answer: A: Yes, it’s completely okay. Calming down takes time, especially when emotions are strong. Don’t pressure yourself to feel better instantly. Breathe, give yourself space, and do one small thing that helps, like walking, journaling, or resting. Be patient and kind with yourself. Every little step you take is progress. Here is a short video on how to calm your mind. Here is a podcast on how to manage difficult emotions.

question: Why am I so scared of dying?! Why does the thought of death make me anxious?
expected_answer: A: Fear of death is natural; your brain is wired to protect you. But often, this fear is really about the unknown or unfinished parts of life. Gently bring yourself to the present. What can you do today that brings peace, meaning, or connection? That’s where calm begins. This short video on overcoming anxiety can help calm your anxiety. Here is a mini-podcast to help you overcome the fear of death.

question: Is it normal to fear death even when I’m young?
expected_answer: A: Yes. Death makes us face life’s uncertainty, and that can feel overwhelming at any age. Instead of pushing the fear away, try noticing it without judgment. It often softens when we explore what truly matters to us, and live from that place. Here are some breathing exercises to help you feel more calm and gain clarity. Here is a podcast to help deal with our own Mortality.

question: How can I stop obsessing over dying?
expected_answer: A: When fear becomes a loop, it helps to ground yourself in the here and now. Breathe. Touch something real. Shift focus to what brings you happiness. Talking about your fears with someone you trust or journaling about them can also release the weight of them. Watch this short video on overcoming fear. Explore this Guided program on the Fear of dying for more insights. Use this online journal to write down your thoughts.

question: What’s a healthy way to think about death?
expected_answer: A: Think of death not as something to fear, but as a reminder to live more intentionally. It teaches us to value time, be present, love more deeply, and let go of what doesn’t matter. When we focus on kindness, connection, and purpose, we honor life and make peace with its end. Watch this short video on overcoming anxiety. Here’s a Guided program on how an awareness of death can help us celebrate life.

question: How can I think about death without feeling anxious?
expected_answer: A: Think of death not as something to fear, but as a reminder to live more intentionally. It teaches us to value time, be present, love more deeply, and let go of what doesn’t matter. When we focus on kindness, connection, and purpose, we honor life and make peace with its end. Watch this short video on overcoming anxiety. Here’s a Guided program on how an awareness of death can help us celebrate life.

question: Is it okay to be scared of losing someone I love?
expected_answer: A: It’s natural to feel this way, & yes, deeply caring makes the idea of loss painful. It shows how much someone means to you. Try to channel that love into the moments you share now. Presence can ease fear. Watch this short video on how to deal with anxiety. Listen to this meditation on releasing anxiety. Explore this guided program on how to deal with the fear of death.

question: I fear losing my loved one.
expected_answer: A: It’s natural to feel this way, & yes, deeply caring makes the idea of loss painful. It shows how much someone means to you. Try to channel that love into the moments you share now. Presence can ease fear. Watch this short video on how to deal with anxiety. Listen to this meditation on releasing anxiety. Explore this guided program on how to deal with the fear of death.

question: How can I support a friend who is scared of losing someone?
expected_answer: A: Just be there. Let them talk. Don’t rush to fix their fear; hold space for it. A simple “That makes sense, I’m here for you” can mean everything. Listen to this audio meditation on the importance of listening. Here is a podcast on exploring mortality with more ideas on how to support others.

question: Why do I feel anxious after someone else’s death, even if I wasn’t close?
expected_answer: A: Any loss can awaken deeper fears. It reminds us of our own mortality and loved ones. Give yourself space to feel and process your emotions. It’s okay to be affected. Listen to this podcast on overcoming grief that will give you a new perspective. Try this audio meditation on how to release anxiety. Listen to these beautiful soundscapes to feel more calm.

question: Can I be a good parent even when I feel anxious or unsure?
expected_answer: You’re not alone, many loving parents feel uncertain or overwhelmed. It’s easy to wonder if doubt means you’re doing something wrong but feeling anxious doesn’t make you a bad parent. In fact, the very act of worrying shows how much you care. The life story I am a ‘good’, anxious mother captures this beautifully, while the podcast Parenting with wisdom offers deeper reflections and encouragement when things feel shaky.

question: Why do small things hurt so much in my relationship with my child?
expected_answer: Emotional moments between parents and children often carry more weight than we realize. When your bond matters deeply, even small ruptures can feel big. That’s why emotional exchanges with your child can hit with surprising force. The short story Being on daddy’s naughty list hurt explores this from a child’s view, while the podcast Communicating effectively with kids offers insights on rebuilding trust and understanding.

question: How do I build stronger communication with my kids—especially when they shut down?
expected_answer: It starts with small acts of listening and showing that you care. When kids shut down, it’s often because they feel unheard, misunderstood, or overwhelmed so rebuilding communication starts with quiet presence and patience. Instead of rushing to fix or advise, try simply being available: showing you’re there when they’re ready. Listen to this podcast Communicating effectively with kids, which walks through challenges and emotional barriers.

question: Why does it feel so hard to talk to my teenager sometimes?
expected_answer: Teenagers are figuring out who they are, and that journey often looks like withdrawal or resistance. It’s their way of learning independence, not rejecting love.The video 5 parenting tips offers bite-sized support, while the podcast Raising teenagers gives you the bigger picture and lots of useful guidance.

question: What are some simple tips to be a better parent today?
expected_answer: Even the best parents have messy days, and being a “better” parent doesn’t mean being perfect, it means being present. Sometimes small changes like validating your child’s feelings or pausing before responding make a big impact. The short video Be better parents offers quick inspiration, and the video on Parenting with wisdom expands on how parenting evolves through presence, not perfection.

question: How do I stay patient when parenting feels overwhelming?
expected_answer: It helps to remember that patience doesn’t mean never getting frustrated, it’s about how you return to calmness. But staying patient doesn’t mean never getting irritated; it means learning how to pause, breathe, and respond with intention instead of reaction. Parenting asks you to grow alongside your child, not just guide them. The video Sakshi Singla on parenting shares small coping tips, and the video on Parenting Teenagers with wisdom offers deeper strategies for navigating this emotional journey.

question: What do I need to understand about parenting teens right now?
expected_answer: Teenagers thrive when they feel trusted and supported, even when they push boundaries. They’re craving trust, respect, and a sense of autonomy while still needing emotional anchors at home. Listen to this podcast Raising teenagers to explore what they really need from you as they grow.

question: How do I know if I’m being too strict or too lenient with my teenager?
expected_answer: It’s not easy to strike the right balance between boundaries and freedom. Your instincts matter, but so does reflection. This short video 5 parenting tips offers practical guidance, and the longer video on Parenting Teenagers with wisdom helps you explore where your approach sits and how to adjust with empathy. Listen to this podcast on Setting boundaries for teens.

question: How do I know I’m growing as a parent, even when it feels hard?
expected_answer: Growth doesn’t always feel good in the moment but if you’re reflecting, adapting, and trying again, you’re evolving. Watch the video Be better parents for quick motivation, and dive into the story I am a ‘good’, anxious mother to see growth through self-awareness and love.

question: What can help me feel more confident as a parent?
expected_answer: Confidence as a parent isn’t about having all the answers, it’s about showing up, staying curious, and building trust with your child, one moment at a time. When you move from control to connection, everything shifts. The story Being on daddy’s naughty list hurt gently reveals how even simple interactions can have emotional weight. For a deeper dive into relational skills, the podcast Communicating effectively with kids explores practical ways to listen, respond, and stay emotionally attuned.

question: What is HappierMe?
expected_answer: HappierMe is a wellness app with separate sections for teens and adults. It helps you deal with your challenges now and then go deeper to understand your own mind, to avoid and overcome them more easily in the future. Many users find it life changing. It is your guide to lead a happier and more purposeful life. Here is a 1-min introduction

question: How do I get started with HappierMe?
expected_answer: Start with this Guide to HappierMe. Begin here. It has videos and audios which help you understand what the app does, and how to make the most of it.

expected_answer: It is up to you, but 10 minutes/day will be enough to begin experiencing the benefits. You could just do the ‘My daily practice’ every day.

question: What is self-awareness?
expected_answer: Watch this short video on self-awareness. Self-awareness is a simple way of noticing what you are thinking and feeling and doing, and then to be curious and understand why. It empowers you to manage your mental wellbeing, have stronger relationships and make better decisions. It also leads to emotional intelligence which can contribute to your success in life.

question: How can the app help me with my mental health?
expected_answer: By understanding our thoughts, feelings and emotions better, we can better manage our reactions, and so manage our own mental health. Here is a link to the mental section of the app.

question: How can the app help me to have happier relationships?
expected_answer: By understanding ourselves, our reactions and our own emotional needs we can understand others better, and this can help us to reduce conflict in our relationships. By learning to communicate with care we can have relationships with depth and meaning. By learning how to love ourselves and others, we can invite more love into our lives, and we can feel less lonely. Explore the Relationship section here. Listen to these beautiful soundscapes to feel more calm.

question: How can the app help me to avoid addiction and build better habits?
expected_answer: Many of our habits which can lead to addiction begin in our thinking, but we are not aware of them. One of them is our need to escape from our suffering, which we do not know how to deal with. Listen to these beautiful soundscapes to feel more calm. By understanding our own mind we can find more intelligent ways of dealing with it and this can help us avoid and overcome addiction. Explore the overcoming addiction section here.

question: How can the app help me succeed at work?
expected_answer: Studies suggest that our soft skills play a much greater role in our long term success than academic achievement. They include emotional intelligence, communication, leadership, empathy, resilience and integrity. All of these are enhanced by self-awareness. Explore the Work and Leadership section here. Listen to these beautiful soundscapes to feel more calm.

question: Does HappierMe offer meditations?
expected_answer: There is a library of audio meditations included in the app. More are added regularly. There is also a Guided program on Meditation with guidance on a good meditation practice. Listen to these beautiful soundscapes to feel more calm.

question: I want to know more about your live events
expected_answer: We host live events on different subjects. There is usually an invited guest and an opportunity to contribute and ask questions. You can access our library of past events here.

question: What is your partnership program?
expected_answer: Once you subscribe you can join our partnership program, and be rewarded for sharing HappierMe with your network. Details can be found in the Partnership program.

question: How can I contact a coach through the app?
expected_answer: Contact our trained coaches here. They are familiar with the app and can offer 1-2-1 coaching for an extra fee. You can ask a coach a question as part of your subscription through the Community Forum here, and this can be done anonymously.

question: Is there a community forum?
expected_answer: The app has a community forum for you to ask questions, make new friends and share your thoughts. There is also a feature where you can ask one of our trained coaches a question anonymously.

question: How can I track my progress?
expected_answer: There is a built-in Wellness survey in the app which helps you to track your progress. You get points and certificates as you go through the app.

question: I want to know more about your teenage program
expected_answer: Teenagers are under so much pressure and need support to manage their mental health, make sense of their emotions, and develop the soft skills to succeed in life. Here is a link to the teen app.

expected_answer: Our mission is to help reduce suffering, help people lead happier lives, and make the world a better place. Here is a summary. This is why we created HappierMe.

question: I love my partner, but we argue all the time. How can we stop fighting so much?
expected_answer: Arguments are natural but frequent conflict can damage trust. Watch the short podcast on Why do we argue to understand the root cause of disagreements. Explore the Guided program on Communication for long-term strategies. Try a breathing exercise to regulate emotions before difficult conversations.

question: How do I know if this relationship is actually good for me?

Journaling on your needs and values may provide clarity.

question: My partner shuts down when we talk about serious things. What should I do?

question: How can I rebuild trust after it’s been broken?
expected_answer: It’s frustrating when someone avoids emotional conversations. Watch the short video Tips to build healthy relationships to understand how to build trust. Try the audio meditation Releasing fear to help you approach sensitive topics more gently. You can also explore this Guided program on Love for more insights.

question: I feel like my partner doesn’t really listen to me — how can I get through to them?
expected_answer: Feeling unheard can be very lonely. Watch the podcast on How can I learn to listen and speak better. Explore the Guided program on Listening for more tips. You can also try these breathing exercises to feel grounded before speaking.

question: What are some simple things we can do to feel more connected?

expected_answer: There is no relationship without conflict - the key lies in how you navigate those differences. Watch this short video on Dealing with conflict. Listen to this podcast on Healing relationships to help you overcome relationship conflicts and strengthen your relationships.

question: How can I deal with relationship conflict?
expected_answer: There is no relationship without conflict - the key lies in how you navigate those differences. Watch this short video on Dealing with conflict. Listen to this podcast on Healing relationships to help you overcome relationship conflicts and strengthen your relationships.

question: Is it normal to feel bored in a long-term relationship? What can we do about it?
expected_answer: Yes, relationships go through phases. It’s normal and it’s important to talk about it and work through it. Try a breathing exercise to help you both reset together.

question: How do I tell someone I have feelings for them without ruining the friendship?
expected_answer: Vulnerability can be scary but also rewarding. Watch the short video on Should I tell someone I like them to help you make the right decision. Watch this Conversation on Dating Tips for more insights. Take your time and lead with honesty and care.

question: My parents don’t understand me, and we always clash. How do I handle that?

question: How can I talk to my kids without losing my patience?
expected_answer: Parenting under pressure is hard. Watch the short video on Be better parents for tips on staying grounded and present. question: I feel like I’m failing as a parent. Can you help me be better?
expected_answer: A: You are not alone and many parents feel this way. Practicing calm breathing can help you respond rather than react when talking to kids. Listen to the podcast on Raising teenagers for insights into parenting through emotional shifts.

question: What’s the best way to deal with arguments in the family?
expected_answer: Family arguments often come from unspoken emotional needs. Watch the short video Having an argument by Martha Van Dam for strategies to stay calm and connected, even when things get heated. Explore this Guided program on Emotional needs for more insights.

question: How can I stay calm when my child is having a meltdown?
expected_answer: A: Your calm energy helps them feel safe. Try the guided breathing exercises to regulate your response. You can also try these Tapping exercises as another way of keeping calm. Even just one minute of mindful breathing can make a big difference.

question: My teenager doesn’t talk to me anymore. How do I reconnect?

question: I feel like I give everything to my family but get nothing back. What should I do?

question: I don’t have many close friends. How do I build deeper connections?

question: My friend hurt me, but I’m not sure if I should bring it up. What’s the right thing to do?

question: I feel left out in my friend group — how do I deal with that?

question: I struggle to get along with my co-workers. How can I improve that?
expected_answer: A: Workplace dynamics can be tricky. Listen to the podcast on Workplace relationships for tips on building stronger relationships.

question: My boss makes me feel small in meetings. How should I respond?
expected_answer: A: That can take a toll on your confidence. Self-awareness can help you shift the way you think and feel and not let other people’s behaviour affect you so much. Explore these Guided exercises on self-awareness for more details. Listen to the podcast on How can I accept criticism without getting hurt to help shift your mindset.

question: How can I give feedback at work without sounding rude or passive-aggressive?
expected_answer: A: Giving honest feedback with care is a skill. Explore this Guided Program on Criticism to learn how to offer feedback with care. Read this short story of offering critical feedback.

question: I get anxious before difficult conversations at work. Any advice?
expected_answer: A: That kind of anxiety is completely normal. Try this guided meditation Releasing fear before conversations to settle your nerves. The Guided program on Communication can also help you communicate calmly and clearly.

question: How do I stay calm in workplace conflicts?
expected_answer: A: Emotional regulation is key. Watch this short video on Dealing with conflict to learn how to respond, not react. Practicing simple breathing exercises can help you stay grounded in the moment.

question: Why do I always feel the need to be right in arguments?
expected_answer: A: That urge often comes from a deeper need to be heard or validated. Watch this short video on Managing an argument. Listen to the podcast on Why do I feel the need to be listened to and be important to explore what’s underneath the surface. Explore this guided program on opinions and beliefs for deeper insights.

question: I get overwhelmed in emotional situations. How can I stay grounded?
expected_answer: A: When emotions flood in, it helps to pause and breathe. Watch this short video from our coach Martha Van Dam on Feeling overwhelmed. Try this guided meditation on Body scan to shift into a calmer state. Even a few minutes can create space for clarity and compassion.

question: I feel lonely even though I’m surrounded by people. What’s going on?
expected_answer: A: That’s more common than you think. It’s probably linked to the depth of connection you have with others. Listen to the mini podcast Why do I feel lonely to understand the root of this feeling. Explore the Guided program on Loneliness for long-term support.
